terms.definition: A notification that something happened (e.g., button click, mouse move, timer tick).
terms.example: // ActionEvent fired when a JButton is clicked

terms.definition: An object that registers interest in certain events and provides callback methods.
A method invoked by the framework when a corresponding event occurs.
terms.example: public void actionPerformed(ActionEvent e) { /* handle click */ }

terms.definition: An event representing an action on an interactor (e.g., button press, Enter in text field) or a timer tick.
An event carrying mouse details such as coordinates and button state.
terms.example: public void mousePressed(MouseEvent e) { int x = e.getX(); }

terms.definition: An event representing keyboard input (press, release, type).
terms.example: public void keyPressed(KeyEvent e) { int code = e.getKeyCode(); }

terms.definition: A UI control (e.g., JButton, JTextField) that can generate ActionEvents.
A string associated with an ActionEvent, often the button text or a custom command.
The act of subscribing to events so callbacks will be invoked.
Framework-managed loop that dequeues events and dispatches them to listeners.
terms.example: // Managed by ACM/Swing; you provide handlers only

terms.definition: Generates periodic ActionEvents at a fixed delay, useful for animation.
terms.example: Timer t = new Timer(20, this); t.start();

terms.definition: Base class for graphical objects on the GCanvas.
Determining which GObject is under a given point.
Stored dx, dy between mouse press point and object origin to enable smooth dragging.
terms.example: dx = e.getX() - obj.getX(); dy = e.getY() - obj.getY();

terms.definition: The object that fired the event (e.g., a specific JButton or Timer).
